title: China-to-Ethiopia Solar PV Inverter Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.requirement: ECAE Pre-Export Verification of Conformity (PVoC) for Solar PV Inverters
rows.target_requirement: The Ethiopian Conformity Assessment Enterprise (ECAE) administers the Pre-Export Verification of Conformity (PVoC) programme, which requires regulated products to be inspected, tested, and certified in the country of export before shipment to Ethiopia. A Certificate of Conformity (CoC) issued by an ECAE-authorised inspection body is required for customs clearance of in-scope product categories. The programme was expanded in May 2024 under Regulation No. 549/2024 to cover additional product categories beyond the initial solar focus. Stand-alone solar products ≤ 350 W have been covered since February 2022; exporters of grid-connected inverters should verify whether their product category and power rating fall within the current PVoC scope with ECAE or an authorised inspection body before shipment.
target_standards: Ethiopian Conformity Assessment Enterprise establishment and PVoC programme
ES IEC TS 62257-9-8:2020 (stand-alone solar products, Ethiopian adoption)
IEC 62109-1 and IEC 62109-2 (safety of power converters for PV systems — referenced for inverter safety evidence)
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese inverter manufacturers typically hold NB/T 32004 type-test reports, GB/T 19964 grid-connection test records, and CGC or TÜV safety certificates. These domestic documents support technical due diligence but are not accepted as substitutes for the Ethiopia ECAE PVoC CoC at the port of import.
china_standards: IEC 62109-1 / IEC 62109-2 (where held — supports PVoC application)
rows.gap: Engage an ECAE-authorised inspection body before the shipment schedule is set. Confirm whether the inverter power rating and product category are in PVoC scope under the expanded 2024 programme. If in scope, arrange pre-export inspection and testing in China; obtain the CoC before goods are released. IEC 62109-1 and IEC 62109-2 test reports strengthen the PVoC application and may be required by the inspection body.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for regulated product categories under the ECAE PVoC programme; scope of grid-connected inverters should be confirmed with ECAE or an authorised inspection body
mandatory_status.cn: No equivalent mandatory pre-export verification for domestic Chinese market; exporters must comply with destination-country conformity requirements

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Confirm the PVoC scope with ECAE or an authorised inspection body before shipment. If in scope, a CoC is required for customs clearance. IEC 62109 test evidence strengthens the application.

source.publisher: TÜV Rheinland (ECAE-authorised inspection body, Ethiopia PVoC programme overview)

rows.requirement: Ethiopian Energy Authority (EEA) Grid Code and EEU Connection Requirements
rows.target_requirement: The Ethiopian Energy Authority (EEA) is the electricity and renewable-energy regulator responsible for the national grid code. The Ethiopia National Transmission Grid Code (ENTGC) and Ethiopia National Distribution Grid Code (ENDGC) set technical requirements for voltage, frequency, protection, and disconnection that inverter-connected generation must satisfy. Ethiopian Electric Utility (EEU) is the distribution utility and the practical connection counterparty for many distributed and commercial solar projects. Ethiopia operates at 220 V / 50 Hz. Renewable power plants must remain connected within the POC operating voltage band and within the 49.0–50.2 Hz frequency range. Grid-connected inverter projects require EEA licensing and EEU connection review; a product-level safety certificate alone does not constitute grid-connection approval.
target_standards: Ethiopian Energy Authority (EEA) licensing and regulatory instruments
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese grid-connection evidence is typically based on GB/T 19964-2024, GB/T 37408, and NB/T 32004, with utility-specific parameter settings for the Chinese grid (50 Hz nominal, but with different voltage tolerance bands, reconnection timers, and protection relay settings). These records do not establish EEA licensing status or EEU connection approval for an Ethiopia project.
rows.gap: Prepare Ethiopia-specific inverter settings: 50 Hz nominal, 220 V single-phase / 380 V three-phase, protection relay thresholds, anti-islanding logic, and reconnection timing per the ENTGC/ENDGC. Engage EEA and EEU early for project licensing and connection review. Build a connection pack with single-line diagrams, protection co-ordination, commissioning plan, and Ethiopian project-specific documentation.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for grid-connected projects under EEA licensing rules and EEU connection requirements; off-grid systems follow different electrical safety and project requirements
mandatory_status.cn: Chinese domestic grid approval is not accepted as EEA or EEU connection approval for Ethiopia projects

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Separate the product import file from the Ethiopia project approval file. EEA and EEU decide grid-connection eligibility; a PVoC CoC or Chinese grid certificate alone does not approve generation or export to the EEU grid.

source.publisher: Encyclopedia MDPI — Grid Code Requirements in Ethiopia (academic summary of ENTGC/ENDGC)

rows.requirement: IEC 62109 Safety Evidence for Solar PV Inverters
rows.target_requirement: IEC 62109-1 (general safety requirements) and IEC 62109-2 (particular requirements for inverters) are the internationally recognised safety standards for solar PV power converters. These standards are referenced by ECAE authorised inspection bodies for PVoC conformity assessment of solar inverter products imported into Ethiopia. Holding a valid third-party test report or certificate to IEC 62109-1 and IEC 62109-2 from an accredited laboratory materially strengthens a PVoC application and is commonly required by project owners and EPC contractors for grid-connected installations.
target_standards: IEC 62109-1:2010 — Safety of power converters for use in photovoltaic power systems — Part 1: General requirements
IEC 62109-2:2011 — Safety of power converters for use in photovoltaic power systems — Part 2: Particular requirements for inverters
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese PV inverters are commonly certified to NB/T 32004 (inverter technical specification) and carry CGC or TÜV test reports. Where the manufacturer also holds an IEC 62109-1 / IEC 62109-2 report from an accredited body, that report directly supports the Ethiopia PVoC application. NB/T 32004 alone is not treated as equivalent to IEC 62109 by ECAE-authorised inspection bodies.
china_standards: IEC 62109-1 / IEC 62109-2 (where held by the Chinese manufacturer)
rows.gap: Obtain a current IEC 62109-1 and IEC 62109-2 test report or certificate from an accredited laboratory covering the specific inverter model. Confirm scope (input voltage range, output voltage, power rating, isolation type) matches the Ethiopia-market configuration before relying on an existing certificate.

mandatory_status.target: Voluntary as a standalone standard but practically mandatory for PVoC CoC issuance by ECAE-authorised inspection bodies and for project-owner acceptance in grid-connected installations
mandatory_status.cn: NB/T 32004 is the common domestic equivalent; IEC 62109 is voluntary in China but required for many export markets

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] IEC 62109-1 and IEC 62109-2 test evidence is practically required for Ethiopia PVoC clearance and project acceptance. Verify that the certificate covers the exact model and Ethiopia market configuration.
